1. # **Introduction** {#introduction}

   1. ## **Purpose** {#purpose}

   This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) document defines the functional and non-functional requirements for FlashLearn, a web-based English vocabulary learning system utilizing SM-2 algorithm for spaced repetition. The purpose of this document is to ensure that all the features and requirements of FlashLearn are well-defined and systematically tested before release.

   The document will serve as a foundation for developers, testers, and stakeholders to understand and implement the application’s requirements. It will also guide the development and testing processes, ensuring that the system meets the needs of its users.

   2. ## **Scope** {#scope}

   This document covers all software requirements for FlashLearn, a web-based vocabulary learning platform for individual learners. FlashLearn enables users to manage flashcards, decks, and track their progress. The core feature of the system is its use of the SM-2 spaced repetition algorithm to optimize review intervals based on user performance. The application supports CSV import/export and progress tracking.

   3. ## **Intended Audiences and Document Organization** {#intended-audiences-and-document-organization}

   This document is intended for the following audiences:

* Development Team: Responsible for implementing the system and ensuring all functional and non-functional requirements are met.

* Testing Team: Responsible for verifying that FlashLearn functions as expected through various testing activities (unit, integration, system testing).

* Project Stakeholders: Includes project managers, business analysts, and any other stakeholders involved in the review and feedback of the requirements.


  4. # **References** {#references}

| \# | Title | Version | File Name / Link | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | ***FlashLearn Algorithm*** | 1.0 | [FlashLearn\_Algorithm](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Rur8wenztKvoFxn9YAgIHhdncfp-4woCZSN2A9uogk0/edit?usp=sharing)f | Defines the **SM-2 algorithm** used in FlashLearn for spaced repetition. |
|  |  |  |  |  |


2. # **Functional Requirements**

   1. ## **Use Case Description** {#use-case-description}

      1. ### **UC-01: Register (FR-1.1)** {#uc-01:-register-(fr-1.1)}

| Name | Register |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows a new user/learner to create an account by providing necessary personal information. |
| **Actor** | Unregistered User (Learner) |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks on the "Sign Up" or "Register" button on the landing page. |
| **Pre-condition** | The user is not currently logged in. |
| **Post-condition** | A new user account is created in the database.  The user is redirected to the Login page or Dashboard. |

   #### **Activities Flow**

![][image1]

## *Figure 1: Register Activities Flow*

#### **Business Rules**

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2)* | *BR1* | Loading Screen Rules: The system loads "Registration" screen. (Refer to "Registration Screen" list in "List Description" file) |
| *(5, 6, 7, 8, 9\)* | *BR2* | Submission Rules: When user clicks on "Submit" on the Registration screen, the system will validate inputted information. If isValidEmail(email) and isValidUsername(username) and isValidUsername(password) and isUnique(username) and isUnique(Email) then the system will process the data as the following bcrypt.hash(password) userRepository.save(username, hashedPassword, email) The system displays MSG02. Else, the system displays MSG01.  |

2. ### **UC-02: Login (FR-1.2)** {#uc-03:-logout-(fr-1.3)}

| Name | Login |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows a registered user to access the system by authenticating their credentials. |
| **Actor** | Registered User (Learner) |
| **Trigger** | When the user opens the application or clicks "Login". |
| **Pre-condition** | The user has a valid account. |
| **Post-condition** | A user session (token) is created.  The user is redirected to the Dashboard. |

   #### **Activities Flow**

   ![][image2]

   ## *Figure 2: Login Activity Flow*

   #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(3,4,5,6,7)* | *BR3* | When user clicks on "Login", if authenticateUser(email, password) then  generateJWT(user)  grantSession(jwtToken)  The system redirects the user to "Dashboard". Else, MSG03 error. |

   3. ### **UC-03: Logout (FR-1.3)** {#uc-03:-logout-(fr-1.3)}

| Name | Logout |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows the user to end their current session securely. |
| **Actor** | Logged-in User |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks the "Logout" button. |
| **Pre-condition** | The user is currently logged in. |
| **Post-condition** | The active session is terminated.  The user is redirected to the Login interface. |

   #### **Activities Flow**

   ![][image3]

   *Figure 3: Logout Activity Flow*

   #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-1}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2,3,4)* | *BR4* | When user clicks “Log out” The system will perform clearJWTToken(), redirects to Login screen and prevents “Back” navigation |

      4. ### **UC-04: View User Profile (FR-1.4)** {#uc-04:-view-user-profile-(fr-1.4)}

| Name | View User Profile |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | Allows the logged-in user to view their own account details. |
| **Actor** | Logged-in User |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks on "Profile" or "Settings". |
| **Pre-condition** | The user is logged in. |
| **Post-condition** | User details (username, email, stats) are displayed. |

   #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow}

   ![][image4]

   *Figure 4: View User Profile Activity Flow*

   #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-2}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2,3,4)* | *BR5* | **Loading Screen Rules:** When user accesses Profile, the system fetches user data via Session ID. The system displays \[Username\], \[Email\], and \[Stats\]. (Refer to "Profile Screen" list in "List Description" file) |

      5. ### **UC-05: Update User Profile (FR-1.5)** {#uc-05:-update-user-profile-(fr-1.5)}

| Name | Update User Profile |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | Allows the user to update their account information, such as email or password. |
| **Actor** | Logged-in User |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks "Save Changes" on the Profile page. |
| **Pre-condition** | The user is logged in. |
| **Post-condition** | User record is updated in the database. |

   #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-1}

   ![][image5]

   *Figure 5: Update User Profile Activity Flow*

   #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-3}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2,3,4)* | *BR6* | **Profile Update Rules:** When user clicks "Save Changes": If passwordFieldIsModified() then bcrypt.hash(newPassword) userRepository.update(newUsername, newEmail, newPassword) The system returns a success message MSG04. |

### 

      6. ### **UC-06: Delete User Account (FR-1.6)** {#uc-06:-delete-user-account-(fr-1.6)}

| Name | Delete User Account |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | Allows the user to permanently delete their account and all associated data. |
| **Actor** | Logged-in User |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks "Delete Account". |
| **Pre-condition** | The user is logged in. |
| **Post-condition** | User and all their decks/cards are removed. Session is terminated. |

   #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-2}

   ![][image6]

   *Figure 6: Delete User Account Activity Flow*

   #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-4}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(4,5,6,7)* | *BR7* | **Account Deletion Rules:** When user clicks "Delete Account" on an account, the system displays a confirmation dialog MSG05. If user clicks "Confirm", the system performs the following cascade delete transaction: deckRepository.deleteAllDecks(userID) cardRepository.deleteAllCards(userID)	 reviewRepository.deleteAllReviews(userID) userRepository.deleteUser(userID) logout() Else (User clicks "Cancel"), the system closes the dialog and takes no action. |

      7. ### **UC-07: View Deck Library (FR-2.1)** {#uc-07:-view-deck-library-(fr-2.1)}

| Name | View Deck Library |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | Displays a list of all flashcard decks belonging to the user. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user visits the Dashboard. |
| **Pre-condition** | The user is logged in. |
| **Post-condition** | A list of decks (titles, descriptions, card counts) is displayed. |

   #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-3}

   ![][image7]

   ## *Figure 7: View Deck Library Activity Flow*

   #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-5}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2,3,4)* | *BR8* | **Loading Screen Rules:** The system queries DB for User's Decks. The system parses rich content fields (JSON) for display. The system loads "Dashboard" screen with the list of Decks. (Refer to "Dashboard" list in "List Description" file) |

      8. ### **UC-08: Create Deck (FR-2.2)** {#uc-08:-create-deck-(fr-2.2)}

| Name | Create Deck |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows the user to create a new container (deck) for flashcards. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks the "Create New Deck" button on the Dashboard. |
| **Pre-condition** | The user is logged in. |
| **Post-condition** | A new deck is added to the database and associated with the current user. |

   #### **Activities Flow**

   ![][image8]

   ## *Figure 8: Create Deck Activity Flow*

   #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-6}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2)* | *BR9* | **Loading Screen Rules:** The system loads "Create Deck" screen. (Refer to "Create Deck Form" list in "List Description" file) |
| *(2,3,4)* | *BR10* | Deck Saving Rules: When user clicks on "Save" on the "Create Deck" form, the system validates the input. If isFieldEmpty(deckName), the system shows a validation error message MSG06. Else, the system saves the item as the following: If isFieldEmpty(languageMode) then languageMode \= "VN\_EN" deckRepository.save(deck) The system refreshes the "Deck List". |

      9. ### **UC-09: Edit Deck (FR-2.3)** {#uc-09:-edit-deck-(fr-2.3)}

| Name | Edit Deck |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows the user to modify the details of an existing deck. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks the "Edit" icon/button on a specific deck. |
| **Pre-condition** | The user is logged in and owns the deck. |
| **Post-condition** | The deck's name or description is updated in the database. Cards inside remain unchanged. |

   #### **Activities Flow**

   ![][image9]

   *Figure 9: Edit Deck Activity Flow*

   #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-7}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2)* | *BR11* | **Loading Screen Rules:** The system loads "Edit Deck" screen with details populated. (Refer to "Edit Deck Form" list in "List Description" file) |
| *(2,3,4)* | *BR12* | **Deck Update Rules:** When user clicks "Update", the system updates the DB record deckRepository.save(newDeck) The system displays a success notification MSG07. The system returns to "Deck List". |

   

      10. ### **UC-10: Delete Deck (FR-2.4)** {#uc-10:-delete-deck-(fr-2.4)}

| Name | Delete Deck |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows the user to permanently remove a deck and all its contents. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks the "Delete" button on a specific deck. |
| **Pre-condition** | The user is logged in and the deck exists. |
| **Post-condition** | The deck and all associated cards are removed from the database. |

   #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-4}

   ![][image10]

   ## *Figure 10: Delete Deck Activity Flow*

   #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-8}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2)* | *BR13* | **Deletion Rules:** When user clicks "Delete" on a deck, the system displays a confirmation dialog MSG08. If user clicks "Confirm", the system performs the following cascade delete transaction: reviewRepository.deleteAllReviews(deckID) cardRepository.deleteAllCards(deckID) deckRepository.deleteDeck(deckID) The system updates the UI to remove the deck. Else (User clicks "Cancel"), the system closes the dialog and takes no action. |

      11. ### **UC-11: View Deck Statistics (FR-2.5)** {#uc-11:-view-deck-statistics-(fr-2.5)}

| Name | View Deck Statistics  |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows users to view basic review statistics for a deck, including the percentage of correct reviews and detailed counts by quality. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user selects a deck and requests to view its statistics. |
| **Pre-condition** | User is authenticated Deck exists in the system User owns the deck |
| **Post-condition** | User views basic performance metrics for the selected deck. |

   #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-5}

   ![][image11]

   *Figure 11: View Deck Statistics Activity Flow*

   #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-9}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2,3,4)* | *BR14* | **Deck Statistics Aggregation Rules:** The system aggregates data across all cards in the deck and calculates:  Total reviews across all cards calculateTotalReviews(.ALL) Correct reviews count and percentage calculateTotalReviews(.CORRECT);calculateTotalReviewsPercentage(.CORRECT) Quality distribution counts (Again, Hard, Good, Easy)calculateQualityDistribution(.ALL) |

### 

### 

12. ### **UC-12: View Advanced Deck Statistics (FR-2.5)** {#uc-12:-view-advanced-deck-statistics-(fr-2.5)}

| Name | View Advanced Deck Statistics |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows users to view comprehensive deck-level statistics including card distribution, maturity data, retention rates, and study patterns. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user selects a deck and requests to view advanced statistics. |
| **Pre-condition** | User is authenticated Deck exists in the system User owns the deck |
| **Post-condition** | User views comprehensive metrics for the selected deck including card distribution, maturity levels, and study consistency. |

#### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-6}

#### **![][image12]**

*Figure 12: View Advanced Deck Statistics Activity Flow*

#### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-10}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2)* | *BR15* | **Advanced Statistics Calculation Rules:** The system calculates and displays: Card distribution by status (new, learning, review, relearning) calculateCardDistribution() Mature cards (interval ≥ 21 days) vs young cards calculateMatureYoungCards() Cards due today and next 7 days calculateNumberOfDueCards(today); calculateNumberOfDueCards(nextWeeks) Overall retention and completion rates calculateRetentionRates();calculateCompletionRates(); Average ease factor and interval calculateAverageEaseFactor();calculateEaseInterval(); Consecutive days studied calculateConsecutiveDaysStudied(); Estimated review time based on card velocity calculateEstimatedReviewTime(); |

13. ### **UC-13: Browse Deck Cards (FR-3.1)** {#uc-13:-browse-deck-cards-(fr-3.1)}

| Name | Browse Deck Cards |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | Displays all cards within a specific deck for management. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks on a deck title to view its contents. |
| **Pre-condition** | The deck exists. |
| **Post-condition** | A list of cards (Front/Back) is displayed. |

    #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-7}

    ![][image13]

    *Figure 13: Browse Deck Cards Activity Flow*

    #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-11}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2,3,4)* | *BR16* | **Loading Screen Rules:** When user opens a Deck, the system queries DB for Cards. The system parses the \[examples\] JSON string for each card. The system displays the list of Cards (Front/Back). (Refer to "Deck Details" list in "List Description" file) |

    14. ### **UC-14: Add Card (FR-3.2)** {#uc-14:-add-card-(fr-3.2)}

| Name | Add Card |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows users to add a new flashcard to a specific deck. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks "Add Card" inside a specific deck view. |
| **Pre-condition** | The target deck exists. |
| **Post-condition** | A new card is created with initial SM-2 learning parameters. |

    #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-8}

    ![][image14]

    *Figure 14: Add Card Activity Flow*

    #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-12}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2)* | *BR15* | **Loading Screen Rules:** The system loads "Add Card" screen. (Refer to "Add Card Form" list in "List Description" file) |
| *(6,7,8,9)* | *BR16* | **Card Saving Rules:** When user clicks "Save", the system performs the following: The system converts \[examples\] list into a JSON string. setDefaultSM2Value() The system saves the card. If \[Deck Language Mode\] \= "BIDIRECTIONAL", the system will perform reverseCards(). |

        15. ### **UC-15: Edit Card (FR-3.3)** {#uc-15:-edit-card-(fr-3.3)}

| Name | Edit Card |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows users to modify the content of an existing card. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user selects a card and clicks "Edit". |
| **Pre-condition** | The card exists. |
| **Post-condition** | The card content (Front/Back) is updated. Learning progress is NOT reset. |

        #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-9}

    ![][image15]

    *Figure 15: Edit Card Activity Flow*

    #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-13}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2)* | *BR17* | Loading Screen Rules: The system loads "Edit Card" screen with data populated. (Refer to "Edit Card Form" list in "List Description" file) The system parses \[examples\] JSON string to object for display. |
| *(2)* | *BR18* | Card Update Rules: When user clicks "Save" on the "Edit Card" form, the system performs the following: The system converts the \[examples\] object back to a JSON string. The system updates the DB record with new content. The system preserves existing Learning Progress values (\[Interval\], \[Repetitions\], \[E-Factor\]). |

    ### 

        16. ### **UC-16: Delete Card (FR-3.4)** {#uc-16:-delete-card-(fr-3.4)}

| Name | Delete Card |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows users to remove a specific card from a deck. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks "Delete" on a card. |
| **Pre-condition** | The card exists. |
| **Post-condition** | The card is permanently removed from the database. |

        #### **Activities Flows** {#activities-flows}

    ![][image16]

    *Figure 16: Delete Card Activity Flow*

    #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-14}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2)* | *BR19* | **Deletion Rules:** When user clicks "Delete" on a card, the system displays a confirmation dialog MSG09. If user clicks "Confirm", the system performs the following cascade delete transaction: reviewRepository.deleteAllReviews(cardID) cardRepository.deleteCard(cardID) The system updates the UI to remove the Card. Else (User clicks "Cancel"), the system closes the dialog and takes no action. |

        17. ### **UC-17: View Card Statistics (FR-3.5)** {#uc-17:-view-card-statistics-(fr-3.5)}

| Name | View Card Statistics |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows users to view comprehensive statistics for a specific flashcard, including review history, retention rates, and performance metrics. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user selects a card and requests to view its statistics. |
| **Pre-condition** | User is authenticated Card exists in the system User has access to the card |
| **Post-condition** | User views detailed performance metrics for the selected card. |

        #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-10}

    ![][image17]

    *Figure 17: Delete Card Activity Flow*

    #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-15}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2)* | *BR20* | **Card Statistics Calculation Rules:** The system aggregates data across all cards in the deck and calculates:s Total reviews across all cards Correct reviews count and percentage Quality distribution counts (Again, Hard, Good, Easy) |

    

        18. ### **UC-18: Import JSON (FR-4.1)** {#uc-18:-import-json-(fr-4.1)}

| Name | Import JSON |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows users to bulk-create cards by uploading a JSON file. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks "Import JSON" in the deck menu. |
| **Pre-condition** | A valid JSON file exists on the user's device. |
| **Post-condition** | Multiple cards are created in the selected deck. |

    #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-11}

    ![][image18]

    *Figure 18: Import Activities Flow*

    #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-16}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2)* | *BR21* | **Loading Screen Rules:** After parsing JSON, the system loads "Import Preview" screen. (Refer to "Import Preview List" list in "List Description" file) |
| *(2)* | *BR22* | **JSON Import Rules:** When the user clicks "Confirm Import", the system processes the items. While more items exist: If isCardValid(card), the system will perform cardRepository.create(card) Else, the system skips the invalid item. The system completes the import process. |

        19. ### **UC-19: Export JSON (FR-4.2)** {#uc-19:-export-json-(fr-4.2)}

| Name | Export JSON |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows users to download all cards from a deck as a JSON file. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks "Export JSON" in the deck menu. |
| **Pre-condition** | The deck contains at least one card. |
| **Post-condition** | A JSON file is downloaded to the user's device. |

    #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-12}

    ![][image19]

    *Figure 19: Export JSON Activity Flow*

    #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-17}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| *(2)* | *BR23* | **JSON Export Rules:** When user clicks "Export JSON" on a deck: The system retrieves all \[Cards\] for \[Deck ID\]. The system serializes the card list to a JSON string. The system triggers a browser download of the .json file. |

        20. ### **UC-20: Start Study Session (FR-5.1)** {#uc-20:-start-study-session-(fr-5.1)}

| Name | Start Study Session |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case initializes a study session by fetching cards that are due for review. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks the "Study Now" button on a deck. |
| **Pre-condition** | The deck contains cards that are due for review. |
| **Post-condition** | A list of cards is retrieved and the user enters the study interface. |

    #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-13}

    ![][image20]

    ## *Figure 20: Start Study Session Activity Flow*

    

    #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-18}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---- |
| *(2,3,4)* | *BR25* | **Study Logic Rules:** When user clicks "Study Now" on a deck, the system retrieves "Due Cards" from the database. The system filters cards where isNewCard(card) The system will perform sortCards() The system displays the \[Front\] side of the first card in the queue. |

        21. ### **UC-21: Record Review Outcome (FR-5.2)** {#uc-21:-record-review-outcome-(fr-5.2)}

| Name | Record Review Outcome |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case processes the user's feedback (Again, Hard, Good, Easy) to schedule the next review. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user clicks a feedback rating button after flipping a card. |
| **Pre-condition** | A study session is active and a card is currently displayed. |
| **Post-condition** | The card's interval, ease factor, and next review date are updated in the database. |

    #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-14}

    ![][image21]

    ## *Figure 21: Record Review Outcome Activity Flow*

    #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-19}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| *(4,5,6,7)* | *BR26* | **Review Calculation Rules:** When user clicks "Show Answer", the system displays \[Back\] side and Rating buttons. When user selects a Rating (Again/Hard/Good/Easy), the system calculates new schedule values using "SM-2 Algorithm". The system performs the following transaction: cardRepostory.update(card) reviewRepository.create(review) If remainingCards \> 0, loadNextCard(card) Else, the system initiates the Session Summary process. |

        22. ### **UC-22: Session Summary (FR-5.3)** {#uc-22:-session-summary-(fr-5.3)}

| Name | Session Summary |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case displays performance statistics immediately after a study session ends. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user completes the review of the last card in the session queue. |
| **Pre-condition** | All cards in the current session have been reviewed. |
| **Post-condition** | The user views a summary report and returns to the Dashboard. |

    #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-15}

    ![][image22]

    ## *Figure 22: Session Summary Activity Flow*

    #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-20}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| *(2)* | *BR27* | **Summary Calculation Rules:** When the last card review is completed, the system aggregates session data. calculateAccuracy() The system displays "Summary Screen" with \[Total Reviewed\], \[Ratings Count\], and \[Accuracy\]. When user clicks "Finish" or "Home", the system redirects the user to "Dashboard". |

        23. ### **UC-23: View Study Session Statistics (FR-5.3)** {#uc-23:-view-study-session-statistics-(fr-5.3)}

| Name | View Study Session Statistics |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Description** | This use case allows users to view statistics for a specific study session defined by a time range, including cards reviewed, accuracy, and quality distribution. |
| **Actor** | User |
| **Trigger** | When the user completes a study session or requests session statistics for a specific time period. |
| **Pre-condition** | User is authenticated Deck exists in the system User owns the deck Valid date range is provided |
| **Post-condition** | User views performance metrics for the specified study session. |

    #### **Activities Flow** {#activities-flow-16}

    ![][image23]

    ## *Figure 23: View Study Session Statistics Activity Flow*

    #### **Business Rules** {#business-rules-21}

| Activity | BR Code | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| *(2)* | *BR28* | **Session Date Range Rules:**  The system requires both startDate and endDate parameters in ISO 8601 format. The time range should represent a single study session. |
| *(4)* | *BR29* | **The system calculates and displays:** Total cards reviewed in the session calculateTotalReviews(SESSION) New cards introduced, learning cards, and review cards counts calculateNewCardsCount() Correct and incorrect answer counts calculateAnswerCounts(.CORRECT);calculateAnswerCounts(.INCORRECT); Accuracy percentage calculateAccuracy() Total study time and average time per card calculateTotalStudyTime(); calculateAverageTime(card); Quality distribution (Again, Hard, Good, Easy counts) calculateQualityDistribution(.ALL-) |

3. # **Non-functional Requirements** {#non-functional-requirements}

   ## **User Access and Security**

| Function / Data | Guest | Learner (User) | Administrator |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Manage “User Account”** |  |  |  |
| Register (Create) | X |  | X |
| Read Profile |  | X(\*) | X |
| Update Profile |  | X(\*) | X |
| Delete Account |  | X(\*) | X |
| **Manage “Deck”** |  |  |  |
| Create |  | X |  |
| Read |  | X(\*) | X |
| Update |  | X(\*) |  |
| Delete |  | X(\*) | X |
| **Manage “Card”** |  |  |  |
| Create |  | X(\*) |  |
| Read |  | X(\*) | X |
| Update |  | X(\*) |  |
| Delete |  | X(\*) | X |
| **Study Activities** |  |  |  |
| Start Session |  | X(\*) |  |
| Submit Review |  | X(\*) |  |
| View Stats |  | X(\*) | X |
| **Data Operations** |  |  |  |
| Import JSON |  | X(\*) |  |
| Export JSON |  | X(\*) |  |

* **X**: User has full permission to do the action.  
* **X(\*)**: User has permission to do the action on their own items (Data Isolation).

4. # **System Requirements** {#system-requirements}

## **Custom Pages** {#custom-pages}

| \# | Page Name | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | User Dashboard | The main landing page for Learners, displaying study statistics (due cards), deck overviews, and navigation to study sessions. |
| 2 | Study Session Interface | The interactive view for flashcard review using the SM-2 algorithm, allowing users to rate their recall performance (Again, Hard, Good, Easy). |
| 3 | Deck Management | A comprehensive list view allowing users to create, edit, delete, and organize decks, as well as import/export data. |
| 4 | Statistics  | A detail page for Learners to view learning statistics include: Accuracy, Learning Streak, Number of Deck. |

5. # **Appendixes** {#appendixes}

   ## **Glossary** {#glossary}

The list below contains all the necessary terms to interpret the document, including acronyms and abbreviations.

| Term | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| *BR* | **B**usiness **R**ule |
| *CBR* | **C**ommon **B**usiness **R**ule |
| *DB* | Notes **D**ata**b**ase |
| *MSG* | **M**es**s**a**g**e |
| *UC* | **U**se **C**ase |
| *N/A* | **N**ot **A**vailable or **N**ot **A**pplicable, used to indicate when information in a certain section could not be provided because it does not apply to this application. |
| *UI* | **U**ser **I**nterface |
| *SRS* | **S**oftware **R**equirements **S**pecification |
| *TBD* | **T**o **b**e **d**etermined or **t**o **b**e **d**efined |

## 

## **Messages**

This section describes the details of messages used in business rules e.g. error messages, confirmation messages, etc.

| Message Code | Message Content | Button |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| MSG01 | Validation failed during registration (e.g., invalid format, non-unique username/email). |  |
| MSG02 | Successful user registration.	 |  |
| MSG03 | Invalid login credentials (Email/Password).	 |  |
| MSG04 | Successful update of the user profile.	 |  |
| MSG05 | “Are you sure you want to delete your account?"	 | OK/Cancel |
| MSG06 | Deck creation validation error: Deck Name is empty. |  |
| MSG07 | "Are you sure you want to delete this deck?" | OK/Cancel |
| MSG08 | "Are you sure you want to delete this card?" | OK/Cancel |
